name: Builders & LBPs
description: Licensed Building Practitioners for new builds, renovations, extensions, and structural work.
licensing: LBP (Licensed Building Practitioner) licence required for restricted building work on homes and small buildings.
avg_cost_nz: $80–$120/hr for labour, or fixed-price contracts for new builds
what_to_look_for: Valid LBP licence (check the public register)
Public liability insurance (ask for a certificate)
Clear written quote with inclusions and exclusions

name: Plumbers & Gasfitters
description: Licensed plumbers and gasfitters for water supply, drainage, hot water, and gas fitting work.
licensing: Must be registered and licensed under the Plumbers, Gasfitters and Drainlayers Act 2006. Check the PGD Board register.
avg_cost_nz: $90–$130/hr (higher for after-hours callouts)
what_to_look_for: Current registration with the PGD Board
Licensed for the specific work type (plumbing, gasfitting, or drainlaying)
Upfront pricing or clear hourly rate
red_flags: Quotes suspiciously low for gas work

name: Electricians
description: Registered electrical workers for wiring, switchboards, heat pumps, EV chargers, and all electrical installations.
licensing: Must be a Registered Electrical Worker (REW) under the Electricity Act 1992. All electrical work must be certified.
avg_cost_nz: $80–$120/hr plus materials
what_to_look_for: Issues an Electrical Certificate of Compliance (CoC) after work
Experience with your specific job type (domestic, commercial, heat pumps)
red_flags: Doesn't mention a Certificate of Compliance
Quotes for work without seeing the site

name: Painters & Decorators
description: Professional painters for interior and exterior residential and commercial painting, wallpapering, and decorating.
licensing: No mandatory licensing in NZ, but look for members of the Painting and Decorating Association NZ (PDANZ).
avg_cost_nz: $55–$80/hr, or $400–$700/day. Full interior house repaint: $3,000–$8,000+
what_to_look_for: References and photos of recent work
Detailed quote specifying paint brand, number of coats, preparation work
Experience with your surface type (weatherboard, plaster, etc.)
red_flags: Won't specify paint brand or quality

name: Roofers & Roof Tilers
description: Roofing contractors for metal roofing, concrete tiles, membrane roofing, re-roofing, and leak repairs.
licensing: No mandatory licensing, but look for members of the Roofing Association of NZ (RANZ). LBP required for structural roof changes.
avg_cost_nz: Full re-roof: $8,000–$25,000+ depending on size and material. Repairs: $300–$2,000
what_to_look_for: Experience with your specific roof type (Colorsteel, concrete tile, membrane)
Product and manufacturer warranties supplied in writing
red_flags: Asks for full payment before work begins
Can't show photos of similar past jobs

name: Landscapers & Garden Designers
description: Professional landscapers for garden design, planting, lawn installation, paving, irrigation, and outdoor living spaces.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Look for members of Landscape NZ (formerly NZILA/LAND8).
avg_cost_nz: $65–$95/hr for labour. Full garden makeover: $5,000–$80,000+ depending on scope
what_to_look_for: Knowledge of NZ native plants and local soils
Clear scope with plant species and quantities specified
red_flags: No plant species specified in quote
No soil preparation included in quote

name: Tilers
description: Tile layers for floors, walls, bathrooms, kitchens, and outdoor areas. Includes waterproofing and grout work.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Waterproofing must comply with NZ Building Code E3.
avg_cost_nz: $80–$120/hr. Floor tiling: $90–$150/m² installed. Wall tiling: $100–$170/m²
what_to_look_for: Experience with your tile type (large format, mosaic, outdoor)
Adhesive and grout product details in quote
red_flags: No waterproofing mentioned for wet areas
Can't specify adhesive or grout products
Quote per m² only — no detail on prep

name: Plasterers
description: Interior and exterior plasterers for plasterboard stopping, texture coatings, Rockcote, and heritage lime plaster work.
licensing: No mandatory licensing in NZ.
avg_cost_nz: $35–$60/m² for standard plasterboard stopping. Exterior texture coatings: $25–$55/m²
what_to_look_for: Experience with your specific system (GIB, plaster, texture coat)
Knowledge of E2 (External Moisture) requirements for exterior work
red_flags: Can't specify the product system being used
No experience with exterior cladding waterproofing

name: Carpet Layers & Flooring Specialists
description: Specialists in carpet, vinyl, LVP, engineered timber, laminate, and hardwood floor installation.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Look for members of the NZ Flooring Association (NZFA).
avg_cost_nz: Carpet: $18–$30/m² installed. LVP: $25–$45/m² installed. Engineered timber: $35–$60/m²
what_to_look_for: Experience with your specific floor type
red_flags: Per m² quote only with no detail

name: Concreters
description: Concrete specialists for driveways, paths, slabs, footings, decorative concrete, and concrete cutting.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. LBP required for structural concrete work on buildings.
avg_cost_nz: Plain concrete driveway: $110–$160/m² installed. Exposed aggregate: $140–$200/m². Decorative: $180–$350/m²
what_to_look_for: NZ cement specification in quote (strength, finish)

name: Carpenters & Joiners
description: Skilled carpenters for decking, stairs, interior joinery, door hanging, window installation, and custom timber work.
licensing: LBP required for structural carpentry on residential buildings.
avg_cost_nz: $80–$110/hr. Custom decking: $600–$1,200/m² installed. Interior fit-out: $85–$120/hr
what_to_look_for: LBP licence for any structural work
Portfolio of custom joinery or decking projects
Experience with your specific project type
Clean finish and attention to detail
red_flags: Can't provide LBP number for structural work

name: Gasfitters
description: Licensed gasfitters for LPG and natural gas installations, appliance connections, and gas line work.
licensing: Must be licensed under the Plumbers, Gasfitters and Drainlayers Act 2006. Gas work requires a specific gasfitting licence.
avg_cost_nz: $90–$130/hr. Gas cooker connection: $200–$450. New gas line installation: $500–$2,500+
what_to_look_for: Experience with your gas type (LPG or natural gas)
Issues a Gas Certificate of Compliance
Familiarity with AS/NZS 5601 gas installation code
red_flags: Can't show gasfitting licence (not just plumbing)
Won't provide a Certificate of Compliance
Quotes very low for gas line work

name: Drainlayers
description: Licensed drainlayers for stormwater, sewer connections, septic systems, French drains, and drainage design.
licensing: Must be registered and licensed under the Plumbers, Gasfitters and Drainlayers Act 2006.
avg_cost_nz: $90–$130/hr. Simple drain connection: $800–$2,500. Septic system: $8,000–$20,000+
what_to_look_for: Drain camera inspection before any major work
red_flags: Doesn't mention council approval for connections

name: Glaziers & Glass Specialists
description: Glass installation and replacement for windows, doors, shower screens, balustrades, and commercial glazing.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Must comply with NZ Building Code Clause F4 (Safety from Falling).
avg_cost_nz: Window replacement (double-glazed): $400–$900 per unit. Shower screen: $800–$2,500. Glass balustrade: $350–$700/m
what_to_look_for: Glass specification (toughened, laminated, double-glazed) in writing
Building Code F4 compliance for safety glass locations
Correct weather seals and flashing included
red_flags: Standard float glass in safety locations
No mention of building code compliance

name: Fencers
description: Fencing contractors for timber, steel, Colorsteel, pool fencing, and rural fencing.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Pool fencing must comply with the Fencing of Swimming Pools Act 1987.
avg_cost_nz: Timber paling fence: $150–$280/m installed. Colorsteel: $200–$350/m. Pool fence (glass): $300–$600/m
what_to_look_for: H treatment grade for in-ground posts (H4 or H5)
Pool fence compliance certificate if applicable
red_flags: H3 posts for in-ground use (must be H4/H5)
No pool fence compliance for pool enclosures

name: Waterproofers
description: Specialist waterproofing contractors for bathrooms, decks, roofs, basements, and below-ground structures.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Must comply with NZ Building Code Clause E2 and E3.
avg_cost_nz: Bathroom waterproofing: $800–$2,500. Deck membrane: $80–$180/m². Roof membrane: $60–$130/m²
what_to_look_for: Specific membrane system named (Sika, Ardex, Mapei etc)
Primer and detailing spec in quote
red_flags: Experience only with residential (not your commercial job type)

name: Insulation Installers
description: Insulation specialists for ceiling, underfloor, and wall insulation. Includes Warmer Kiwi Homes-approved providers.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Warmer Kiwi Homes-registered assessors required for grants.
avg_cost_nz: Ceiling insulation: $20–$32/m² installed. Underfloor: $20–$28/m²
what_to_look_for: R-value specification matching your climate zone
Warmer Kiwi Homes registration if claiming grants
Proper coverage including eaves and edges

name: Heat Pump Installers
description: HVAC specialists for heat pump supply and installation, multi-split systems, and ducted systems.
licensing: Must be a Registered Electrical Worker (REW). Refrigerant handling requires an additional certificate.
avg_cost_nz: Single room unit (supply + install): $2,500–$4,500. Multi-split system: $8,000–$18,000+
what_to_look_for: Correct sizing calculation for your room
5+ year warranty on parts and labour

name: Solar Panel Installers
description: Solar PV specialists for residential and commercial solar panel installation, battery storage, and EV chargers.
licensing: Must be a Registered Electrical Worker (REW). Grid-connected systems require notification to lines company.
avg_cost_nz: 5kW system: $12,000–$16,000 installed. 10kW: $20,000–$28,000. Battery storage: add $8,000–$14,000
red_flags: Generation guarantee not offered in writing

name: Scaffolders
description: Scaffolding contractors for residential and commercial access scaffolding, roof edge protection, and mobile scaffolding.
licensing: No mandatory licence but scaffolders must be competent under the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015.
avg_cost_nz: Residential scaffold (erect + 4 weeks): $2,000–$5,500. Commercial: $25–$50/m² erected
what_to_look_for: Tagged scaffold with load rating displayed
red_flags: Won't certify the scaffold before use
No edge protection on steep roofs

name: Demolition Contractors
description: Specialist demolition for residential and commercial buildings, internal strip-outs, asbestos remediation, and site clearing.
licensing: Asbestos removal over 10m² requires a Class B or Class A asbestos removal licence from WorkSafe.
avg_cost_nz: Internal strip-out: $3,000–$12,000. House demolition (small): $15,000–$35,000+. Asbestos removal: $80–$200/m²
what_to_look_for: Asbestos survey before any demolition on pre-2000 buildings
WorkSafe asbestos removal licence if required
red_flags: No asbestos survey offered for pre-2000 buildings

name: Arborists & Tree Services
description: Qualified arborists for tree removal, pruning, stump grinding, tree reports, and emergency storm work.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Look for NZ Arboricultural Association (NZAA) certified arborists.
avg_cost_nz: Small tree removal: $500–$1,500. Large tree: $2,000–$8,000+. Stump grinding: $200–$600
what_to_look_for: Council consent for protected or notable trees
Full clean-up and chip removal included in quote
red_flags: Quote includes felling but not removal
Not NZAA certified for complex work

name: Kitchen Renovators
description: Kitchen design and renovation specialists for cabinetry, benchtops, appliances, splashbacks, and full kitchen rebuilds.
licensing: LBP required for structural changes. Plumbing changes need a licensed plumber.
avg_cost_nz: Budget kitchen refresh: $15,000–$25,000. Mid-range full renovation: $30,000–$55,000. Premium: $60,000–$120,000+
what_to_look_for: Full scope in writing (cabinetry, benchtop, appliances, plumbing, electrical)

name: Bathroom Renovators
description: Bathroom design and renovation specialists managing tiling, plumbing, waterproofing, fixtures, and fit-out.
licensing: LBP for structural work. Licensed plumber required for plumbing. Building Code E3 for waterproofing.
avg_cost_nz: Basic refresh: $8,000–$15,000. Mid-range full reno: $18,000–$35,000. Premium: $35,000–$80,000+

name: Pest Controllers
description: Licensed pest management professionals for rodents, insects, wasps, possums, and building fumigation.
licensing: Must hold a NZQA Pest Management qualification and comply with the HSNO Act for restricted substances.
avg_cost_nz: Rodent control: $250–$600. Wasp nest: $120–$300. Full building treatment: $400–$1,200+
what_to_look_for: Treatment report and product safety data sheets
red_flags: Won't provide treatment details or product names
Very cheap quote using generic products

name: Security System Installers
description: Security specialists for alarm systems, CCTV, intercoms, access control, and monitored security.
licensing: Must hold a Private Security Personnel and Private Investigators Act 2010 licence for monitoring and patrol services.
avg_cost_nz: Basic alarm system: $1,200–$2,800 installed. CCTV (4 cameras): $1,500–$3,500. Full monitored system: $2,500–$6,000+
what_to_look_for: Private Security licence if providing monitoring
Response time SLA for monitored services
red_flags: Monthly fees not clearly disclosed upfront

name: HVAC Engineers & Air Conditioning
description: Commercial and residential HVAC for ventilation systems, air conditioning, ducted systems, and building automation.
licensing: REW required for electrical work. Refrigerant handling certificate required.
avg_cost_nz: Ducted system (residential): $8,000–$18,000. Commercial HVAC: $150–$400/m² of conditioned space
what_to_look_for: ASHRAE or CIBSE design standards reference

name: Architects
description: Registered architects for residential design, commercial projects, heritage work, and resource consent applications.
licensing: Must be registered with the New Zealand Registered Architects Board (NZRAB).
avg_cost_nz: Residential design: 8–15% of construction cost. Fixed fee for small projects: $5,000–$25,000+
what_to_look_for: Clear scope of services (concept, consents, contract administration)

name: Excavation & Earthworks Contractors
description: Excavation specialists for site preparation, foundations, drainage trenching, retaining walls, and bulk earthworks.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Some earthworks require resource consent under the RMA.
avg_cost_nz: Excavator hire (with operator): $180–$220/hr. Site cut (per m³): $35–$65 disposed. Foundation excavation: $800–$3,000+
what_to_look_for: Correct machine size for your job
Utility location (Call Before You Dig) process
red_flags: No resource consent check for large volumes

name: Steel Fabricators & Structural Steel
description: Structural and architectural steel fabrication for beams, portals, stairs, balustrades, and custom metalwork.
licensing: Structural steel work on buildings requires an LBP licence (Design or Structure category).
avg_cost_nz: Structural steel beam: $120–$250/m installed. Steel portal frame: $35,000–$80,000+. Balustrade: $400–$900/m
red_flags: No engineer approval for structural members

name: Cabinet Makers & Joiners
description: Custom cabinet makers and joiners for kitchens, wardrobes, bathrooms, entertainment units, and commercial joinery.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. LBP required for installation work affecting building structure.
avg_cost_nz: Custom kitchen cabinetry: $1,500–$3,000/m run. Custom wardrobe: $800–$2,500/m. Commercial joinery: varies
what_to_look_for: Board substrate specification (MDF, plywood, particle board)
red_flags: Quote significantly below comparable custom makers

name: Garage Door Suppliers & Installers
description: Supply and installation of panel lift, roller, and sectional garage doors with automated openers.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Building consent required if installing a garage for the first time.
avg_cost_nz: Single roller door (supply + install): $1,800–$3,500. Double panel lift (automated): $3,000–$6,500
what_to_look_for: Wind load rating for your region
Motor brand and warranty (ATA, Merlin, B&D)
Insulated panel option for temperature control

name: Locksmiths
description: Emergency lockouts, lock installation and re-keying, high-security deadbolts, and master key systems.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Building entry requires authorization. Emergency locksmiths operate 24/7.
avg_cost_nz: Standard lockout: $120–$250. New lock supply + fit: $150–$450. Master key system: $600–$2,500+
what_to_look_for: Key control documentation for master systems
red_flags: Extremely high after-hours charges without disclosure

name: Bricklayers & Blocklayers
description: Specialist bricklayers for residential and commercial brick, block, and stone construction.
licensing: LBP required for structural masonry work on residential buildings.
avg_cost_nz: Single-skin brickwork: $120–$180/m² installed. Block wall: $110–$160/m². Heritage restoration: $200–$400/m²
what_to_look_for: Experience with your brick or block type
red_flags: No DPC mentioned for ground-level walls
No movement joints in long walls
Can't provide LBP for structural work

name: Window & Door Installers
description: Supply and installation of aluminium and timber joinery, double glazing, bi-fold doors, and skylights.
licensing: LBP required for installation of new windows and external doors in NZ homes.
avg_cost_nz: Aluminium double-glazed window: $600–$1,500 installed. Full house re-window: $15,000–$45,000+
what_to_look_for: Joinery thermal rating (H-value or window energy rating)
red_flags: Can't specify glass or frame performance ratings

name: Irrigation Specialists
description: Irrigation design and installation for residential lawns, commercial landscapes, and rural properties.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Water takes for irrigation may require resource consent under the RMA.
avg_cost_nz: Residential lawn system: $2,500–$6,000. Drip irrigation (per m²): $8–$18 installed
what_to_look_for: Hydraulic design for correct zone sizing
Water meter and backflow prevention included
Smart controller with soil moisture sensors

name: Pool Builders
description: Swimming pool construction specialists for fibreglass, concrete, and above-ground pools, including fencing and compliance.
licensing: Building consent required for all pools. Pool fence must comply with the Fencing of Swimming Pools Act 1987.
avg_cost_nz: Fibreglass pool: $35,000–$70,000 installed. Concrete/gunite: $50,000–$120,000+. Above-ground: $3,000–$12,000
red_flags: No soil assessment for in-ground pool

name: Asphalt & Driveway Contractors
description: Asphalt, chip seal, and tar seal contractors for driveways, car parks, and road surfacing.
licensing: No mandatory licensing for residential driveways. Road work may require council approvals.
avg_cost_nz: Asphalt driveway: $80–$130/m² installed. Chip seal driveway: $25–$55/m². New sealed road (per km): $300,000+
red_flags: Very cheap quote (thin asphalt, no base)

name: Interior Designers
description: Professional interior designers for residential and commercial space planning, colour schemes, furniture, and fit-out.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Look for members of Spatial Design NZ or NZIA (for architects who do interiors).
avg_cost_nz: Design fee: $100–$180/hr or 10–20% of project value. Full interior design project: $5,000–$30,000+ in fees
what_to_look_for: Clear fee structure (hourly, fixed, or percentage)

name: Land Surveyors
description: Licensed cadastral and topographic surveyors for land subdivision, boundary disputes, and building setout.
licensing: Must be licensed under the Cadastral Survey Act 2002 for cadastral work. Registration with NZIS.
avg_cost_nz: Boundary survey: $1,200–$3,500. Subdivision: $4,000–$25,000+. Building setout: $800–$2,500
what_to_look_for: Licensed cadastral surveyor for boundary/subdivision work
GPS RTK or total station equipment
Title plan and LINZ registration included
red_flags: Using GPS only without total station for precise work

name: Stonemasons
description: Heritage and contemporary stonemasons for natural stone walls, paving, chimneys, and restoration work.
licensing: LBP required for structural stone work. Heritage work may require special consent.
avg_cost_nz: Natural stone wall: $350–$700/m² installed. Stone paving: $200–$450/m². Heritage restoration: $400–$900/m²
what_to_look_for: Portfolio of stone work matching your project
Mortar specification compatible with the stone type
Experience with NZ stone (schist, bluestone, granite)
red_flags: Incompatible mortar mix for stone type
No experience with your stone material
No drainage detail for retaining walls

name: Timber Floor Specialists
description: Hardwood floor installation, sanding, polishing, and restoration for solid and engineered timber floors.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. NZFA (NZ Flooring Association) membership is a quality indicator.
avg_cost_nz: Solid hardwood (supply + install): $150–$280/m². Sanding and polishing: $25–$55/m²
what_to_look_for: Moisture testing of subfloor before installation
red_flags: No acclimatisation period for solid timber

name: Drainage & Stormwater Specialists
description: Stormwater and site drainage design, installation, and maintenance for residential and commercial properties.
licensing: Licensed drainlayer required for sewer connections. Resource consent for stormwater discharge.
avg_cost_nz: French drain: $80–$150/m installed. Soakpit: $1,500–$4,000. Full site drainage: $5,000–$25,000+

name: Cladding Installers
description: Exterior cladding specialists for weatherboard, fibre cement, aluminium composite, brick veneer, and modern facade systems.
licensing: LBP required for cladding installation on residential buildings under the Building Act.
avg_cost_nz: Primed pine weatherboard: $120–$180/m² installed. Fibre cement: $100–$160/m². Brick veneer: $180–$280/m²
what_to_look_for: Compliance with NZ Building Code E2 (External Moisture)
red_flags: No cavity system on weathertight cladding

name: Lighting Designers & Installers
description: Architectural and commercial lighting design, LED upgrades, smart lighting, and outdoor lighting installation.
licensing: Must be a Registered Electrical Worker (REW) for installation. No separate lighting licence required.
avg_cost_nz: Full house LED upgrade (supply + install): $2,500–$6,000. Commercial lighting design: $150–$250/hr. Outdoor lighting: $400–$1,800/circuit
what_to_look_for: REW registration for all installation work
Lux level design for commercial spaces
Smart lighting and DALI control experience
red_flags: Smart system incompatible with your existing platform

name: Commercial & Industrial Cleaners
description: Commercial cleaning contractors for offices, construction site cleans, high-pressure washing, and post-renovation cleaning.
licensing: No mandatory licensing. Chemical handling requires HSNO compliance.
avg_cost_nz: Office cleaning: $30–$55/hr. Construction clean: $2–$5/m². High-pressure washing: $3–$8/m²
what_to_look_for: Chemical safety data sheets for products used
Police vetting for staff with facility access
red_flags: Can't provide product safety data sheets
No police vetting process for keyholders

name: Quantity Surveyors
description: Building cost estimators and construction economists for project budgeting, contract administration, and dispute resolution.
licensing: NZIQS (NZ Institute of Quantity Surveyors) membership is the professional standard.
avg_cost_nz: Construction cost estimate: $2,000–$8,000. Contract administration: 1–3% of construction cost
what_to_look_for: Experience with your project type (residential, commercial, civil)
